name: DAW Transport Bar
description: Studio-grade transport control strip for browser DAWs with playback controls, tempo, key, loop state, metronome, latency, collaborators, and bounce status.

studio.description: Full DAW transport with record, playback, tempo/key, loop, metronome, latency, collaborators, and bounce progress

compact.description: Mobile transport with play/record, current time, and a secondary sheet for advanced controls

visual_brief: Commercial-grade horizontal command strip that reads like studio hardware and a premium shadcn toolbar, not a web navbar. Left cluster has icon-only rewind, play/pause, stop, record, loop, and metronome controls with 40-44px hit targets, clear pressed/armed states, and restrained borders. Center cluster uses tabular monospace timecode, bar/beat position, tempo, key, and a slim progress rail with a precise playhead. Right cluster shows latency, render/bounce readiness, and collaborator avatars. Use cyan for playback/sync, amber for playhead/readiness, red for record, and neutral chrome everywhere else.

slots.controls: Icon-only transport controls. Primary play button is visually dominant; record is red and pulses only when armed.
slots.timeline-status: Current timecode, bar/beat, BPM, key, loop range, and a slim progress rail.
slots.session-status: Latency, render state, sync state, and active collaborators.

composition.Controls: Cluster > [Rewind + PlayPause + Stop + Record + Loop + Metronome]

micro.record-armed: record dot pulses red once per second

ambient.playhead: progress rail advances continuously during playback
ambient.sync-status: latency meter breathes cyan while connected

mobile.notes: Keep play, record, current time, BPM, and collaborators visible. Move secondary toggles into an overflow sheet. Never wrap into a cluttered two-line toolbar unless the layout is intentionally stacked.

responsive.desktop: Full transport remains one row, with status and collaborators visible at all times.

layout_hints.hardware_feel: Transport controls should feel dense and tactile, not like generic text buttons.
layout_hints.status_priority: Timecode and playback state are the primary read targets; collaborator and bounce status are secondary.
